name: Why Was My Workers' Comp Claim Denied? Common Reasons and How to Appeal in Salt Lake City
description: Why Was My Workers' Comp Claim Denied? Common Reasons and How to Appeal in Salt Lake City - Salt Lake City

step.name: Step 1
step.text: Receiving a denial letter for your workers' compensation claim in Salt Lake City is discouraging, but it is not the end of the road. In fact, many legitimate claims are initially denied as a standard tactic by insurance companies to see if the worker will simply give up. If you believe your injury is work-related, you have the right to fight back.

step.name: Step 2
step.text: ### Step 1: Identify the Reason for Denial

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Read your denial letter carefully. The most common reasons include:

step.name: Step 4
step.text: - **Lack of Notice:** The insurer claims you didn't report the injury to your employer in time.

step.name: Step 5
step.text: - **Pre-existing Condition:** They argue your pain is from an old injury, not the work accident.

step.name: Step 6
step.text: - **No Medical Evidence:** They claim your medical records don't clearly link the injury to your job.

step.name: Step 7
step.text: - **Not in the 'Scope of Employment':** They argue you were on a break or performing a non-work task.

step.name: Step 8
step.text: ### Step 2: File an Application for Hearing

step.name: Step 9
step.text: In Utah, the appeal process begins by filing a formal 'Application for Hearing' with the Labor Commission. This document outlines exactly what benefits you are seeking (medical bills, lost wages, etc.) and why you believe the denial was wrong. Once filed, your case moves out of the hands of the insurance adjuster and into the legal system.

step.name: Step 11
step.text: To win an appeal, you need more than just your own word. You will likely need:

step.name: Step 12
step.text: - **Expert Medical Opinions:** A doctor who can explicitly state that the work accident was the 'medical cause' of your current condition.

step.name: Step 13
step.text: - **Witness Statements:** Coworkers who can testify to the conditions of the accident or your physical state immediately afterward.

step.name: Step 14
step.text: - **Incident Reports and Photos:** Concrete proof of the hazard that caused your injury.

step.name: Step 15
step.text: ### Step 4: The Discovery and Hearing Phase

step.name: Step 16
step.text: During the appeal, both sides participate in 'discovery.' This involves a formal exchange of evidence, and you may have to sit for a deposition—a recorded session where the insurance company's lawyer asks you questions under oath. Finally, if a settlement isn't reached, an Administrative Law Judge (ALJ) will hear the case. They will review the evidence and issue a binding decision.

step.name: Step 18
step.text: Navigating a Labor Commission hearing is virtually identical to a court trial. There are rules of evidence, legal deadlines, and complex medical testimony. Insurance companies almost always have experienced attorneys representing them at these hearings. To level the playing field, you should have a lawyer who knows Utah workers' comp law inside and out. At Just Work Comp Law, we handle the heavy lifting of the appeal process so you can focus on getting better.
